name: High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE)
description: A versatile thermoplastic polymer known for its high strength-to-density ratio, excellent chemical resistance, and good impact resistance. Widely used for blow-molded containers and pipings.
specs:
Density: 0.93–0.97 g/cm³
Melt Flow Index: 0.2–30 g/10min
Tensile Strength: 20–37 MPa
Operating Temp: -50°C to 120°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Bottles, pipes, plastic lumber, toys, geomembranes, food containers
imageKeywords: HDPE plastic pellets, polyethylene resin granules, white polymer pellets

name: Low-Density Polyethylene (LDPE)
description: A soft, flexible polymer with excellent processability, transparency and film-forming properties. Lower density and crystallinity compared to HDPE.
specs:
Density: 0.91–0.94 g/cm³
Melt Flow Index: 0.3–50 g/10min
Tensile Strength: 8–30 MPa
Operating Temp: -60°C to 80°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Film packaging, plastic bags, wire insulation, squeeze bottles, coatings
imageKeywords: LDPE transparent pellets, flexible polyethylene granules

name: Linear Low-Density Polyethylene (LLDPE)
description: Combines the toughness of LDPE with higher tensile strength and puncture resistance. Major polymer for stretch films and heavy-duty packaging.
specs:
Density: 0.915–0.925 g/cm³
Melt Flow Index: 0.5–30 g/10min
Tensile Strength: 20–35 MPa
Operating Temp: -60°C to 90°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Stretch film, agricultural film, heavy-duty sacks, liners, tubing
imageKeywords: LLDPE pellets, linear polyethylene resin, packaging film material

name: Polypropylene Homopolymer (PP-H)
description: A rigid, crystalline thermoplastic with excellent stiffness, chemical resistance, and heat resistance. One of the most widely produced plastics globally.
specs:
Density: 0.90–0.91 g/cm³
Melt Flow Index: 3–50 g/10min
Tensile Strength: 30–40 MPa
Operating Temp: -10°C to 130°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Packaging, automotive parts, textiles (non-woven), appliances, caps
imageKeywords: PP plastic pellets, polypropylene granules, white PP resin

name: Polypropylene Copolymer (PP-C / PPR)
description: Offers improved impact resistance and flexibility compared to homopolymer PP, especially at low temperatures. Common in pipe and automotive applications.
specs:
Density: 0.89–0.91 g/cm³
Melt Flow Index: 1–30 g/10min
Impact Strength: High
Operating Temp: -20°C to 120°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Pipes, automotive bumpers, containers, household goods, impact-resistant packaging
imageKeywords: PP copolymer pellets, impact resistant polypropylene, PPR resin

name: Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC Resin)
description: A cost-effective, durable polymer with excellent flame retardancy and chemical resistance. Requires heat stabilizers for processing. Available in rigid and flexible grades.
specs:
Density: 1.35–1.45 g/cm³
K-Value: 55–72 (suspension grade)
Tensile Strength: 40–55 MPa (rigid)
Operating Temp: -15°C to 60°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Pipes, profiles, cables, flooring, window frames, medical tubing, films
imageKeywords: PVC resin powder, white PVC suspension powder, polyvinyl chloride granules

name: Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET)
nameZh: 聚对苯二甲酸乙二醇酯
description: A strong, lightweight, transparent thermoplastic polymer with excellent barrier properties against gases and moisture. Widely used in beverage packaging and fibers.
specs:
Density: 1.33–1.38 g/cm³ (amorphous)
Intrinsic Viscosity: 0.70–0.86 dL/g
Tensile Strength: 50–80 MPa
Operating Temp: -40°C to 70°C
Packaging: 1,100–1,250kg big bags or 25kg
applications: Beverage bottles, food trays, fibers (polyester), films, engineering resins
imageKeywords: PET resin chips, transparent polyester pellets, bottle grade PET

name: Polystyrene General Purpose (GPPS)
description: A rigid, transparent thermoplastic with excellent processability and dimensional stability. Low cost but relatively brittle. Often blended into HIPS or EPS.
specs:
Density: 1.04–1.05 g/cm³
Melt Flow Index: 2–30 g/10min
Tensile Strength: 35–55 MPa
Operating Temp: -20°C to 70°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Disposable cutlery, CD cases, food containers, toys, cosmetic packaging
imageKeywords: GPPS clear pellets, polystyrene granules transparent, crystal PS resin

name: High Impact Polystyrene (HIPS)
description: Modified polystyrene with rubber content to improve impact resistance. Opaque but maintains good processability and printability.
specs:
Density: 1.03–1.06 g/cm³
Melt Flow Index: 1–15 g/10min
Impact Strength: Moderate to High
Operating Temp: -30°C to 80°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Appliance housings, TV cabinets, refrigerator liners, toys, packaging trays
imageKeywords: HIPS white pellets, high impact polystyrene, opaque PS granules

name: Acrylonitrile Butadiene Styrene (ABS)
nameZh: 丙烯腈-丁二烯-苯乙烯共聚物
description: An engineering thermoplastic combining strength, rigidity and excellent impact resistance. Known for its glossy surface finish and good electrical insulation.
specs:
Density: 1.03–1.07 g/cm³
Melt Flow Index: 1–30 g/10min
Tensile Strength: 40–50 MPa
Operating Temp: -40°C to 85°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Automotive interior parts, electronics housings, toys (LEGO), appliances, helmets
imageKeywords: ABS plastic pellets off-white, acrylonitrile butadiene styrene granules

name: Polyamide 6 (PA6 / Nylon 6)
description: A high-performance engineering plastic with excellent abrasion resistance, toughness and self-lubricating properties. Absorbs moisture which affects dimensions.
specs:
Density: 1.12–1.14 g/cm³
Melting Point: ~220°C
Tensile Strength: 70–85 MPa
Operating Temp: -40°C to 120°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Gears, bearings, automotive parts, films, fibers, engineering components
imageKeywords: PA6 nylon pellets translucent, polyamide 6 granules engineering plastic

name: Polycarbonate (PC)
description: An amorphous engineering thermoplastic with outstanding impact strength, optical clarity and heat resistance. More expensive than commodity plastics.
specs:
Density: 1.20–1.22 g/cm³
Melt Flow Index: 3–25 g/10min
Tensile Strength: 55–75 MPa
Operating Temp: -40°C to 135°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Safety glasses, optical lenses, automotive headlamps, electronics, greenhouses, medical devices
imageKeywords: PC transparent pellets, polycarbonate resin clear, engineering plastic granules

name: Polyoxymethylene (POM / Acetal)
description: A crystalline engineering plastic known for high stiffness, low friction and excellent dimensional stability. Often used as a metal replacement in precision parts.
specs:
Density: 1.41–1.42 g/cm³
Melting Point: ~165°C
Tensile Strength: 60–70 MPa
Operating Temp: -40°C to 100°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Gears, bearings, conveyor belts, automotive fuel systems, pumps, valves, zippers
imageKeywords: POM acetal pellets white, polyoxymethylene granules engineering plastic

name: Polyurethane (PU / TPU)
description: A versatile polymer available as rigid foams, flexible foams, elastomers (TPU), coatings and adhesives. Excellent abrasion and tear resistance for elastomer grades.
specs:
Density: 1.10–1.25 g/cm³ (elastomer)
Hardness: Shore A 60A – Shore D 85D
Tensile Strength: 25–55 MPa
Operating Temp: -40°C to 80°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Foam insulation, footwear soles, vehicle seats, hoses, wheels, coatings, adhesives
imageKeywords: TPU pellets transparent amber, thermoplastic polyurethane granules, PU foam material

name: Ethylene Vinyl Acetate (EVA)
description: A copolymer with varying vinyl acetate content. Soft, flexible, with excellent clarity, adhesion and low-temperature toughness. Common in foams and films.
specs:
Density: 0.92–0.95 g/cm³
VA Content: 5%–40%
Melt Flow Index: 0.5–150 g/10min
Operating Temp: -60°C to 65°C
Packaging: 25kg bags
applications: Footwear soles, solar encapsulants, packaging films, toys, hot-melt adhesives, foams
imageKeywords: EVA pellets transparent, ethylene vinyl acetate granules, EVA foam material
